goo: noun. Any semi- solid or liquid substance; especially one that is sticky, gummy or slippery, unpleasant, and of vague or unknown
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
orris: noun. Any of several irises that have a fragrant root, especially Iris × germanica.
our: determiner. Belonging to us, excluding the person(s) being addressed.
ours: pronoun. That or those belonging to us.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rigorous: adjective. Showing, causing, or favoring rigour / rigor; scrupulously accurate or strict; thorough.
rug: noun. A partial covering for a floor.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.

sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
gou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gavar.
grr: interjection. Sound of an animal growl.
grs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gresi.
grus: noun. An accumulation of angular, coarse-grained fragments (particles of sand and gravel) resulting from the chemical and mechanical
gur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Farefare.
iou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tuma-Irumu.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
osi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Osing.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
sog: noun. Soft, marsh -like or bog -like land; a swamp.
sugi: noun. Wood of the Japanese cedar (Cryptomeria japonica).
sur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwaghavul.
urs: noun. The anniversary of the death of a Sufi saint, celebrated with music and ritual.
